comments.body: E1 DESCOPE NOTE (2026-07-16): E1's finding-adjudication TP/FP corpus was INVALIDATED (tool-less, plan-truncated labeling that doesn't match the whole-plan/code-grounded/agentic production gate; ~79 percent contaminated) and DESCOPED — the independent human-adjudicated gold set is parked as idea e59d-c078-724d-46e5. E1 still delivers the VALID outcome corpus + finding sample. This story (R7) uses the E1 OUTCOME-corpus classification method + the frozen §5.2 outcome labels. UNAFFECTED by the adjudication-corpus invalidation; proceed on the outcome corpus when resourced.

compiled_state.description:
## Why
Every gate row and all post-ship dogfooding in this epic needs standing outcome
instrumentation: today nothing routinely ties plan-review verdicts to downstream outcomes
(post-claim edits, reopens, force-closes, verifier FAILs), so false-positive drift stays
invisible until it burns author trust (the Tricorder <10% blocking-FP cliff). E1 froze the
per-ticket **outcome corpus** (`runs/outcome_corpus.jsonl`, 628 rows) and the miner that
produces it; this ticket turns that one-shot artifact into a **re-runnable gate-eval job** that
classifies each reviewed ticket, emits trailing per-criterion FP-proxy metrics, and documents
the alarm/demotion playbook.

## What
Commit a re-runnable **gate-eval instrumentation job** at
`docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/harnesses/gate_eval_instrumentation.py`.

**Corpus acquisition (subprocess, not import).** The job refreshes the outcome corpus by
invoking `mine_outcome_corpus.py` **as a subprocess** (`python <harnesses>/mine_outcome_corpus.py`,
which atomically writes `runs/outcome_corpus.jsonl`), then reads that JSONL. It does NOT
`import` and call `build_corpus()`, because that entry calls `sys.exit()` on floor-check failure
(`mine_outcome_corpus.py:381`), which would terminate the caller; and the miner lives in a
non-package `harnesses/` dir, so subprocess-by-path is also the clean invocation. A
`--no-refresh` flag skips regeneration and reads the committed corpus (offline / CI use). It
then reads each reviewed ticket's persisted `REVIEW_RESULT` sidecars via
`rebar.llm.plan_review.sidecar.all_review_results`.

**Outcome classifier (deterministic; the honest finding is that the sidecar findings do NOT
carry the separating signal).** Investigation against the 8 §5.2 cases proved a classifier over
the sidecar findings *alone* reaches only **4/8** — the CAUGHT-BUT-IGNORED cases (c8cc, f5df)
have finding profiles at or *below* the MISSED cases, because "caught-but-ignored" is a fact
about the author's **post-claim edit**, not about what the gate saw. The separating signal lives
in two outcome-corpus fields plus one sidecar-derived strength predicate. The classifier is a
first-match cascade, applied ONLY to tickets with `had_persisted_review` AND a post-claim signal
(`post_claim_edit_class != "none"` OR `review_round_count >= 2`); all others are **N/A**:

```
C1  post_claim_edit_class == "operator-attested-retag"  → CAUGHT-BUT-IGNORED
C2  review_round_count >= 2                              → CAUGHT-BUT-IGNORED
C3  has_strong_finding                                   → UNKNOWABLE
C4  else                                                 → MISSED
```
- `review_round_count` is the **git-walk outcome-corpus** count (true count), not the pruned
  on-disk sidecar count.
- **`has_strong_finding`** (the exact "substantive advisory" predicate, evaluated over the
  ticket's deduplicated round union — see Multi-round handling): ∃ a finding with
  `decision ∈ {"block","advisory"}` AND `priority >= 0.5` AND `severity ∈ {"critical","major"}`.
  The 0.5 floor is the codebase's own `RECALL_MIN_PRIORITY`
  (`src/rebar/llm/plan_review/sidecar.py:299`); the severity conjunct excludes
  high-priority-but-procedural (P6/P9) findings.

This **core** cascade reproduces the 8 frozen labels at **7/8** (only c8cc, a lone
`substantive-unclassified` case, misclassifies as MISSED). A `substantive-unclassified → CAUGHT`
rule would reach 8/8 but rests on that single case, so it is **deliberately excluded** as
overfitting and documented as such. The README states plainly that the classifier separates via
outcome-corpus fields + a sidecar strength predicate, NOT via sidecar findings alone (which
achieve only 4/8) — the load-bearing methodological finding.

**adverse post-claim edit** (used by both the classifier gate and the metrics below) is defined
once: `post_claim_edit_class ∈ {"plan-authored-post-claim", "ac-strengthened",
"substantive-unclassified"}` (the substantive classes; `operator-attested-retag` is handled by
C1; `cosmetic`/`none` are not adverse).

**Multi-round handling (load-bearing — `all_review_results` returns every retained round).**
`sidecar.all_review_results(ticket_id)` returns ALL retained review payloads (newest→oldest, up
to the 50/ticket cap), not just the latest. For each ticket the harness forms the **deduplicated
union** of findings across all retained rounds, keyed by
`(tuple(sorted(finding["criteria"])), finding["finding"])` — so a finding that persists across
rounds counts exactly **once** (no denominator inflation for high-round tickets) and a finding
cleared in a later round is still counted (no omission of earlier blocks). `has_strong_finding`
is `∃ a strong finding in this union` (consistent with the §5.2 investigation — 8c4f's strong
finding lived in its earlier BLOCK round, cleared by round 2), and every per-criterion metric
denominator below counts C's findings in this union.

**Per-criterion trailing metrics (FINDING-level, matching the parent epic's definition).** Over
the trailing window (see `--window`), joining each ticket's deduplicated finding union to its
outcome row by `ticket_id`, for each criterion C:
- **blocking_fp_proxy(C)** = (# of C's **blocking** findings whose ticket was subsequently
  adjudicated not-block-worthy) / (total # of C's blocking findings). The automatic proxy for
  "adjudicated not-block-worthy" is **`force_close OR reopen_count > 0`** (an explicit gate
  bypass, or a post-close reopen). The earlier `(no adverse post-claim edit)` disjunct is
  **removed**: blocking findings are remediated *pre-claim* in the review loop, so ~95% of
  tickets legitimately have `post_claim_edit_class == "none"`, and that disjunct would fire on
  nearly every honored block and mislabel it FP. This proxy is therefore a deliberately
  conservative **lower bound** (force-close can also happen for orthogonal operator-attestation
  reasons, ADR 0043), so the >10% alarm **triggers human review** of that criterion's recent
  blocks rather than an automatic demotion. Per-FINDING, matching the parent's "fraction of that
  criterion's blocking findings … subsequently adjudicated not-block-worthy."
- **advisory_application_rate(C)** = (# of C's **advisory** findings whose ticket later shows an
  adverse post-claim edit) / (total # of C's advisory findings).
- **rounds_per_ticket** = the `review_round_count` distribution over the window.
- **sample_counts(C)** = `{"blocking_findings": int, "advisory_findings": int, "tickets": int}` —
  the denominators (count of C's blocking findings, C's advisory findings, and distinct tickets
  touching C, over the deduplicated union in the window). Emitted with each criterion so a rate
  computed from a tiny sample is visible rather than hidden behind a bare fraction.

**Empty / degenerate input.** On the live store (628 reviewed tickets) the corpus is always
non-empty; defensively, if the window resolves to zero tickets `--emit` exits non-zero with a
message (rather than writing a trivial `{}`), a criterion with zero findings is omitted, and
`--verify-repro` fails loudly if any of the 8 frozen case ids cannot be resolved.

**`--window N`** bounds the corpus to the last `N` rows in the miner's deterministic emission
order (the corpus has no timestamp field; emission order is the stable proxy, documented as
such — a strictly chronological window is future work needing a timestamp). Default N=200.

**Alarm / demotion rule (documented playbook).** When a criterion's trailing
**blocking_fp_proxy exceeds 10%**, demote it from blocking to advisory via an ordinary
`criteria_routing.json` edit (`default_posture: "advisory"`). Precedent for demotion on FP
evidence: **T5e**, demoted to advisory in calibration-3 (task relishable-ammonitic-hoverfly).
(T3/T10 are a *different* precedent — criteria kept non-blocking from the start; never demoted —
so they are NOT cited as demotion precedent.) Dogfood monitoring, not a config flag; no
gate/source behaviour change.

## Scope
- `docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/harnesses/gate_eval_instrumentation.py` — the job: a pure
  top-level `classify(post_claim_edit_class, review_round_count, has_strong_finding) -> str`
  (unit-testable), the `has_strong_finding` sidecar extractor, the finding-level metrics
  computation, and CLI flags `--verify-repro`, `--emit`, `--window N`, `--no-refresh`.
- `docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/README.md` — classifier rules, the 4/8-vs-7/8 finding, the
  metric definitions + join key, cadence, and the metrics output path.
- `docs/plan-review-gate.md` — the alarm/demotion playbook (>10% blocking-FP rule, T5e
  precedent, operator response). The ONLY change outside `docs/experiments/`; documentation only.
- `tests/unit/test_gate_eval_classifier.py` — a golden test importing the pure `classify` and
  asserting the 8 frozen `(cls, rounds, strong) → label` tuples reproduce ≥6/8, plus a
  non-triviality assertion on a metrics fixture and a `--window` subset check.
- No change to `src/rebar/` behaviour — no gate or source behaviour change.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `--verify-repro` recomputes the classifier over the 8 frozen cases (MISSED: dc58-af7b,
      db7b-c8fd, 5886-d028; CAUGHT-BUT-IGNORED: c8cc-68b8, f5df-0069, 115b-ceea, 8c4f-b81c;
      UNKNOWABLE: 3006-e198), prints the confusion table, and asserts **≥6/8 agreement** (exits
      non-zero below 6/8). Proving command:
      `python docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/harnesses/gate_eval_instrumentation.py --verify-repro`.
- [ ] `--emit` writes per-criterion metrics to
      `docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/runs/gate_eval_metrics.json` — a non-trivial object with
      ≥1 criterion whose `blocking_fp_proxy` and `advisory_application_rate` are **numeric**
      (a float in [0,1], not null) and whose `sample_counts` is a non-empty dict with a
      **positive total** (so the AC is NOT satisfiable by `{}`, all-null values, or all-zero
      sample counts). Proving command:
      `python docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/harnesses/gate_eval_instrumentation.py --emit && python -c "import json;d=json.load(open('docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/runs/gate_eval_metrics.json'));assert d and any(isinstance(v.get('blocking_fp_proxy'),(int,float)) and isinstance(v.get('advisory_application_rate'),(int,float)) and isinstance(v.get('sample_counts'),dict) and sum(v['sample_counts'].values())>0 for v in d.values())"`.
- [ ] The pure `classify` reproduces ≥6/8 on the 8 frozen input tuples in a CI-collected test,
      and `--window N` yields a subset for smaller N. Proving command:
      `python -m pytest tests/unit/test_gate_eval_classifier.py -q`.
- [ ] `docs/plan-review-gate.md` gains a new **"Blocking-FP-proxy demotion"** section
      documenting the >10% `blocking_fp_proxy` alarm rule, the operator demotion response, and the
      correct T5e precedent (not T3/T10, cited inside that section); the harnesses README
      documents the `sidecar-findings-alone` 4/8-vs-7/8 finding. Proving command (both sentinels
      are new content — neither pre-exists in its file, verified by `grep -c`):
      `grep -q "Blocking-FP-proxy demotion" docs/plan-review-gate.md && grep -q "sidecar-findings-alone" docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/README.md`.
- [ ] [operator-attested] `make lint && make typecheck && make test` green; landed through Gerrit (evidence: the Gerrit change id, merge commit, and CI Verified vote recorded in a ticket comment).
compiled_state.title: R7: standing gate-eval outcome instrumentation + dogfood FP monitoring
